_comment: Tier-2 Layer-C: fenics linear_elasticity numerical-correctness gate. Mirrors fenics/poisson_mms_convergence but for a VECTOR-VALUED physics. Plane strain, E=1.0, ν=0.3. Manufactured u = (sin(π·x)·sin(π·y), sin(2π·x)·sin(2π·y)); f computed symbolically via UFL as -div(σ(u)) where σ = 2μ·ε + λ·tr(ε)·I. Exercises the catalog's vector-valued API: basix.ufl.element(Lagrange, cell, k, shape=(gdim,)), fem.dirichletbc on a Function-valued BC, σ/ε Lamé constitutive, LinearProblem with MUMPS. Asserts P1 nx=32 L2 error <= 1.2e-2 (the u_y component oscillates at 2π so resolution per wavelength is half — error doubles vs the unit-wavenumber Poisson case), P2 nx=32 L2 error <= 1e-4, P1 EOC h=1/16 → h=1/32 in [1.7, 2.3]. Verified empirically against dolfinx 0.10.0 + PETSc + MUMPS on 2026-06-01: P1=8.99e-3, P2=5.88e-5, P1 EOC=1.925.
